title: metallodrug_covalent_quantum_3way_cross_v1 — 3-AXIS CROSS rows (METALLODRUG x COVALENT x QUANTUM)
description: Output schema for _python_bridge/module/metallodrug_covalent_quantum_3way_cross.py. This is the repo's FIRST 3-axis cross: the cisplatin Pt(II)-N7(guanine) adduct is simultaneously (a) a METALLODRUG event (d8 square-planar Pt(II) coordination geometry + CFSE; metallodrug_coordination_sim.py), (b) a COVALENT bond-forming event (the Pt-N7 coordinate bond is a 2-centre 2-electron coordinate bond whose Pt-aquation -> Pt-N7-binding step is modelled by Eyring TST per covalent_inhibition_sim.py), and (c) a multireference QUANTUM electronic-structure problem (the Pt 5d / N 2p near-degenerate (2e,2o) CI from metallodrug_quantum_vqe_cross.py). All three parent simulators are IMPORTED, never reimplemented (governance f3). HONESTY: this is a MODEL-LEVEL UNIFICATION — one chemical event admits three valid theoretical descriptions — NOT a claim that metal-coordination, covalent, and electronic-structure modalities are operationally interchangeable in drug design. A 3-axis cross does NOT make a new axis: it specialises the existing axes (:>) and leaves core-5 + expansion-layer UNCHANGED. PASS certifies in-silico simulator+metadata consistency ONLY — never a binding-affinity, therapeutic, cytotoxic, antitumor, immunogenic, efficacy, regulatory, or modality-superiority claim (AGENTS.tape g8/f2). Modalities are described by their own precedent (cisplatin/carboplatin/oxaliplatin) — never lattice-derived (g3/f1/f_lattice_fit).

anchor_event.description: The single chemical event the three axis views describe. All three views must identify the SAME event (cross-axis consistency).

pt_n7_anchor_angstrom.description: Deterministic Pt-N7 coordinate distance anchor (~2.0 A, Takahara 1995). All three axis views key off this same value.

pt_n7_recomputed_angstrom.description: Pt-N radial distance recomputed by metallodrug_coordination_sim.square_planar_geometry at the Pt-N7 anchor.

pt_n7_anchor_match.description: True iff the recomputed Pt-N distance is within the parent module's tolerance of the ~2.0 A anchor (Takahara 1995).

cfse_square_planar_delta_oct.description: Closed-form d8 square-planar CFSE in units of Delta_oct (Griffith & Orgel 1957) — computed by the parent metallodrug sim.

bond_kind.description: The Pt-N7 coordinate bond is a 2-centre 2-electron dative covalent bond. Both lone-pair electrons originate on the N7 donor; the bond is covalent by IUPAC definition (2c-2e).

two_step_mechanism.description: The Pt-aquation -> Pt-N7-binding step formulated in the parent module's two-step covalent kinetics framework (E + I <=> E.I -> E-I).

Ki_molar.description: Surrogate reversible-recognition constant K_i (M) for the Pt-aquo -> Pt-N7 pre-binding step. Illustrative model input, NOT a measured affinity.

kinact_per_s.description: Surrogate first-order rate (1/s) of the irreversible Pt-N7 coordinate-bond formation. Illustrative model input.

dg_covalent_kcal_per_mol.description: Activation barrier (kcal/mol) of the Pt-N7 bond-forming step; Eyring TST barrier proxy.

kinact_eyring_tst_per_s.description: Eyring TST-predicted rate from the supplied dG_double_dagger (parent module's eyring_rate).

dg_implied_from_kinact_kcal_per_mol.description: Eyring TST-implied dG_double_dagger from the supplied kinact (parent module's implied_barrier_kcal).

eyring_prefactor_ceiling_per_s.description: Eyring TST universal frequency prefactor kB*T/h — the hard real-limit ceiling on every modelled rate (Eyring 1935).

kinact_tst_below_eyring_ceiling.description: True iff Eyring TST-predicted rate < eyring_prefactor_ceiling_per_s.

active_space.description: Two electrons in two near-degenerate spatial orbitals — the minimal active space hosting a double excitation.

orbital_pair_label.description: Label of the active orbital pair (e.g. Pt-5d / N-2p frontier pair at the Pt-N7 adduct).

orbital_gap_delta_oct.description: One-electron gap of the active pair, in units of Delta_oct.

coupling_k.description: Determinant-mixing two-electron coupling K (units of Delta_oct) of the (2e,2o) CI.

e_hf.description: Closed-shell HF (single-reference) energy of the (2e,2o) singlet block (anchored at 0).

e_fci.description: Exact FCI ground-state energy of the (2e,2o) singlet block (closed-form 2x2 lower eigenvalue).

e_corr.description: Correlation energy E_FCI - E_HF; must be <= 0 (and strictly < 0 when K != 0).

e_corr_strictly_negative.description: True iff E_corr is strictly < 0 (multireference signature is real).

hf_weight_in_fci.description: |<Phi_HF|Psi_FCI>|^2 — HF determinant weight in the exact CI ground state.

ci_matches_closed_form.description: True iff the exact 2x2 eigenvalue agrees with the closed-form expression (numerical self-consistency).

vqe_dispatch_status.description: Live qmirror/VQE-ladder dispatch is honestly DEFERRED (g7 skip-is-honest) — only the deterministic 2x2 CI is the in-repo PASS object.

same_pt_n7_event_across_axes.description: All three axis views identify the SAME Pt-N7(guanine) coordinate-bond event at the same anchor distance — the cross-axis consistency core.

metallodrug_anchor_passes.description: METALLODRUG view: Pt-N distance matches the ~2.0 A Takahara 1995 anchor.

covalent_eyring_ceiling_respected.description: COVALENT view: kinact and Eyring-predicted rate both respect the kB*T/h ceiling (Eyring 1935).

quantum_e_corr_strictly_negative.description: QUANTUM view: (2e,2o) CI correlation energy is strictly < 0 (multireference signature is real).

all_three_views_consistent.description: Conjunction of the above — the three views agree the Pt-N7 event is the rate-determining / binding event under each respective model.

cross_is_three_axis.description: Always true — this is a 3-axis cross (METALLODRUG x COVALENT x QUANTUM); the repo's first.

creates_a_new_axis.description: Always false — a 3-axis cross does NOT introduce a new axis. The cross specialises (:>) the three existing axes; core-5 + expansion-layer are UNCHANGED.

model_level_unification_disclaimer.description: Mandatory disclaimer: this cross is a MODEL-LEVEL UNIFICATION showing one chemical event admits three valid theoretical descriptions; it is NOT a claim that metal-coordination, covalent, and electronic-structure modalities are operationally interchangeable in drug design.

real_limit_citations.description: At least three literature anchors — one per axis view (METALLODRUG: Takahara 1995, Griffith&Orgel 1957; COVALENT: Eyring 1935, Strelow 2017; QUANTUM: Roos 1980 / Reiher 2017).

lattice_stance.description: Explicit statement that no n=6 lattice arithmetic is performed; modalities are described by their OWN precedent (g2/g3/f1/f_lattice_fit).

in_silico_only.description: Always true — the row certifies in-silico simulator+metadata consistency only (g8/f2); never a therapeutic/clinical/regulatory claim.
